chapter_summary: In chapter 4, Solomon remembers how his own father taught him to love wisdom when he was little. Now he passes that gift on. He says the most important thing you can ever do is get wisdom. Then he gives a very famous command: guard your heart — because everything good in your life flows out of it. He also says to keep your eyes on the right path and not look away to the left or right.
main_lesson: Getting wisdom is the most important thing. Guard your heart and keep your eyes on God's path.
memory_verse: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
parent_guide: Chapter 4 is deeply personal — Solomon is passing on what his own father David taught him. Key themes: (1) Wisdom is a generational gift — parents who model and teach wisdom pass it to their children. (2) 'Get wisdom' (v.7) — this is emphatic in the original Hebrew. The pursuit of wisdom is active and costly. (3) The 'heart' (v.23) in Hebrew thought is the control centre of a person — where decisions are made, desires are felt, and character is formed. Help your child see that what they 'let in' (through their eyes, ears, and friendships) shapes who they become. (4) Verses 25-27 paint a picture of focused, undistracted walking — a powerful metaphor for a life devoted to God.
application_for_children: This week, think about what you are letting into your heart. What are you watching, listening to, and thinking about? Ask God to help you guard your heart and choose things that are good, true, and beautiful.

verses.kjv: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know understanding.
verses.little_bible: Children, listen carefully to what your father teaches you. Pay attention and learn understanding.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Listen to your father. He wants to teach you good things.
verses.meaning: Parents and teachers pass wisdom to us — listening carefully to them is one of the best ways to grow wise.
verses.memory_phrase: I listen and learn from wise people.
verses.prayer: God, give me ears that love to listen and learn.
verses.discussion_question: Who teaches you the most important things in life?
verses.family_discussion: What is one piece of wisdom a grandparent or older person has shared with your family?
verses.do_it_today: Ask someone older than you to share one piece of advice they wish they had known when they were young.
verses.illustration_prompt: A father kneeling beside a small child, both leaning in close together, sharing something special

verses.kjv: For I give you good doctrine, forsake ye not my law.
verses.little_bible: I am giving you good teaching — do not leave it behind or ignore it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Good teaching is a gift. Don't throw it away.
verses.meaning: The wisdom being passed down is genuinely good — worth holding onto and never abandoning. Good teaching is a treasure.
verses.memory_phrase: Good teaching is a treasure.
verses.prayer: God, help me hold onto every piece of good teaching I receive.
verses.discussion_question: What is the best thing someone has ever taught you?
verses.family_discussion: What good teaching has been passed down in your family that you never want to lose?
verses.do_it_today: Write down the best piece of advice you have ever been given.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child carefully placing a glowing scroll into a treasure chest, treasuring it

verses.kjv: For I was my father's son, tender and only beloved in the sight of my mother.
verses.little_bible: I was once a child too — my father's son, dearly loved by my mother.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The wise teacher was once a little child, just like you.
verses.meaning: Solomon reminds us that he too was once a child who received wisdom from his parents. This makes his teaching personal and shows wisdom is for everyone.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom is for children too.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that wisdom is for me — even now while I am young.
verses.discussion_question: Did you know Solomon was once a little child learning from his parents, just like you?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing that even great wise people started as children encourage you?
verses.do_it_today: Tell your parents one thing you have learned from them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young Solomon as a small boy sitting with his father King David, learning together

verses.kjv: He taught me also, and said unto me, Let thine heart retain my words: keep my commandments, and live.
verses.little_bible: My father taught me and said: 'Keep my words in your heart. Follow my commands — and you will live.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep God's words in your heart and live.
verses.meaning: Keeping God's commandments is literally life-giving. This is not just a rule — it is love from a father who wants his child to thrive.
verses.memory_phrase: God's words give me life.
verses.prayer: God, let Your words stay in my heart and give me life.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'keep' a command — not just know it but live by it?
verses.family_discussion: Which of God's commands has made the most difference to your family's life?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one of God's commands and live by it on purpose all day today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A father holding his child's hands, speaking warmly, the child's face glowing with the words being received

verses.kjv: Get wisdom, get understanding: forget it not; neither decline from the words of my mouth.
verses.little_bible: Get wisdom! Get understanding! Do not forget it and do not turn away from these words.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Get wisdom! Get understanding! Don't forget them.
verses.meaning: The repeated command 'get wisdom' is urgent — it is a father's passion that his child pursue wisdom with everything they have.
verses.memory_phrase: Get wisdom! Get understanding!
verses.prayer: God, I want wisdom and understanding more than anything.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think this verse repeats 'get wisdom' and 'get understanding' — what does that tell us?
verses.family_discussion: As a family, how are we actively pursuing wisdom and understanding?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud three times: 'I am getting wisdom today!'
verses.illustration_prompt: An enthusiastic child reaching up eagerly for a glowing crown labelled 'Wisdom' and 'Understanding'

verses.kjv: Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.
verses.little_bible: Do not leave wisdom behind and she will keep you safe. Love wisdom and she will protect you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Love wisdom and she will keep you safe.
verses.meaning: Our relationship with wisdom is like a relationship — if we love it and stay loyal to it, it guards us faithfully.
verses.memory_phrase: I love wisdom and she protects me.
verses.prayer: God, help me love wisdom and never leave it behind.
verses.discussion_question: How is loving wisdom like being loyal to a good friend?
verses.family_discussion: What would it look like for your family to be fiercely loyal to wisdom?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one moment today when you could stay loyal to wisdom instead of giving up on it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking with a gentle glowing figure beside them — wisdom as a faithful companion protecting the path

verses.kjv: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom is the most important thing of all. Do whatever it takes to get it!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom is the best thing to get. Go and get it!
verses.meaning: Out of everything a person could pursue, wisdom is the most valuable. It is worth giving up other things to have it.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom is the best thing.
verses.prayer: God, help me want wisdom more than anything else.
verses.discussion_question: If you could have one thing forever, what would you choose? Is wisdom on that list?
verses.family_discussion: What have you had to give up or work hard for because it was worth it?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to spend 5 minutes reading something wise instead of something just for fun.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child reaching up to catch a glowing star labelled 'Wisdom' — it shines brighter than all the others

verses.kjv: Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her.
verses.little_bible: Lift up wisdom and she will lift you up. Hold wisdom close and she will bring you honour.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Honour wisdom and it will honour you.
verses.meaning: When we hold wisdom in high regard and embrace it fully, it raises us up. Wisdom is not diminished when we honour it — it honours us back.
verses.memory_phrase: Honour wisdom and it will honour you.
verses.prayer: God, I lift up Your wisdom today. Help me hold it high and embrace it.
verses.discussion_question: How do you show that you think something is really important and valuable?
verses.family_discussion: How can our family lift wisdom high in how we live together?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today one wise thing you are going to keep doing this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding up a glowing torch of wisdom, and a warm crown beginning to appear over their head

verses.kjv: She shall give to thine head an ornament of grace: a crown of glory shall she deliver to thee.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom will put a crown of grace and glory on your head. It will make you truly beautiful.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom is like a beautiful crown for your head.
verses.meaning: The inner beauty that wisdom gives is like a crown — it adorns the person with grace and dignity that everyone can see.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom gives me a crown of grace.
verses.prayer: God, let wisdom be my crown — let it make me gracious and good.
verses.discussion_question: What kind of crown is more valuable: a golden one or a crown of wisdom?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like when someone wears wisdom like a crown — how can you tell?
verses.do_it_today: Be gracious and kind today — let wisdom be visible in how you treat people.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child wearing a softly glowing crown made of intertwined light and leaves — wisdom's crown of grace

verses.kjv: Hear, O my son, and receive my sayings; and the years of thy life shall be many.
verses.little_bible: My child, listen to wise teaching and obey it. It will help you live a good and full life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Listen to wise words. They help you live a good life.
verses.meaning: Wisdom is not just for making good choices — it shapes how we live and helps us flourish over a whole lifetime.
verses.memory_phrase: Wise words help me live well.
verses.prayer: God, help me listen to wise teaching and follow it all my life.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between just hearing something and really listening?
verses.family_discussion: Name one wise teaching that has helped your family live better.
verses.do_it_today: Think of one wise thing you have heard this week — put it into action today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A winding path stretching into a beautiful sunrise, with a child confidently walking forward, full of life

verses.kjv: I have taught thee in the way of wisdom; I have led thee in right paths.
verses.little_bible: I have taught you the way of wisdom. I have shown you the right paths to walk on.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A wise teacher shows you the right path.
verses.meaning: A loving parent or teacher who has guided us in wisdom has given us the greatest gift. We should honour that guidance by walking in it.
verses.memory_phrase: I walk in the right paths I have been shown.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for the people who have shown me the right path.
verses.discussion_question: Who has led you in the right path — who helped show you the right way to live?
verses.family_discussion: As parents, what right paths are you leading your children on?
verses.do_it_today: Thank someone today who has taught you or led you the right way.
verses.illustration_prompt: A parent and child walking together on a bright path through a beautiful landscape

verses.kjv: When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitened; and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.
verses.little_bible: When you walk, you will have wide, free steps. When you run, you will not trip.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom gives you room to walk and run without tripping.
verses.meaning: Wisdom does not restrict us — it actually frees us. We walk with more confidence and run without stumbling when wisdom guides our path.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom gives me room to run.
verses.prayer: God, let wisdom free my steps so I can walk and run in Your goodness.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you run in a big open space vs. a cramped room? How is wisdom like that open space?
verses.family_discussion: How does living wisely actually give us more freedom, not less?
verses.do_it_today: Go outside and run freely — thank God that wisdom gives you free, wide steps in life.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child running joyfully across a wide open sunny field, arms spread, completely free and unhindered

verses.kjv: Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: keep her; for she is thy life.
verses.little_bible: Hold onto instruction tightly! Do not let it go — it is your very life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hold on to wisdom! Don't let it go.
verses.meaning: Instruction is not optional — it is life itself. Holding onto it tightly is as important as breathing.
verses.memory_phrase: I hold on to wisdom tightly.
verses.prayer: God, help me hold tightly to Your instruction and never let it go.
verses.discussion_question: If wisdom were a rope on a boat in rough water, why would you hold on very tight?
verses.family_discussion: What instruction or wise teaching is your family holding tightly to right now?
verses.do_it_today: Write a verse on your hand today as a reminder to hold on to wisdom.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child gripping a glowing rope or scroll with both hands, holding on joyfully and confidently

verses.kjv: Enter not into the path of the wicked, and go not in the way of evil men.
verses.little_bible: Do not step onto the path of wrong people. Do not go in the way that evil people walk.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Stay off the path of those who do wrong.
verses.meaning: The first step onto a wrong path is the most dangerous. Wisdom says: do not even begin down that road.
verses.memory_phrase: I don't even start on the wrong path.
verses.prayer: God, keep my feet off the wrong path from the very first step.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important not to even start down a wrong path?
verses.family_discussion: How can we help each other recognise a wrong path before we start on it?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one 'wrong path' you will not even start today — then stay off it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child standing at a fork in the road, confidently choosing the bright path and not even looking at the dark one

verses.kjv: Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it, and pass away.
verses.little_bible: Avoid it! Do not even go near it. Turn away from it and keep walking.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Avoid wrong paths. Turn away and keep going.
verses.meaning: God is emphatic — the best response to a path of evil is not to pause and consider it, but to turn and walk away completely.
verses.memory_phrase: I turn away from wrong.
verses.prayer: God, help me not pause at wrong things but turn and walk away.
verses.discussion_question: Why should you not stop and look at something wrong for a long time?
verses.family_discussion: What does 'turning away and passing on' look like in everyday life?
verses.do_it_today: If you see something wrong today, practise turning away quickly and moving on.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child deliberately turning away from a shadowy path and walking briskly toward the bright one ahead

verses.kjv: For they sleep not, except they have done mischief; and their sleep is taken away, unless they cause some to fall.
verses.little_bible: Some people cannot even rest unless they have done something wrong. Causing harm has become their habit.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some people can only rest after doing wrong — that is a very sad way to live.
verses.meaning: Those who habitually do wrong become consumed by it — their whole lives rotate around mischief. This is a warning about where bad habits lead.
verses.memory_phrase: I rest in what is good.
verses.prayer: God, let my rest come from doing what is right, not from doing harm.
verses.discussion_question: How does doing something right make you feel at bedtime? How does doing something wrong feel?
verses.family_discussion: How do our daily habits shape who we become over time?
verses.do_it_today: Go to bed tonight thinking of one right thing you did today — rest in that.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child peacefully sleeping with a gentle smile, having done what is good all day

verses.kjv: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.
verses.little_bible: Wrong people feed on doing harm — it is like their food and drink. It is all they live on.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Doing wrong becomes like food for people who keep choosing it.
verses.meaning: When wickedness becomes a person's daily diet, it shapes everything they are. What we feed our heart and mind daily shapes who we become.
verses.memory_phrase: I feed on what is good and right.
verses.prayer: God, help me feed on what is good — Your Word, kindness, and truth.
verses.discussion_question: What do you feed your mind and heart every day — good things or bad things?
verses.family_discussion: What is our family's daily 'diet' for our hearts and minds?
verses.do_it_today: Feed your heart something good today — a verse, a kind conversation, or a prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A table with wholesome fruit and bread on one side, and a dark bitter meal on the other — a child choosing the good side

verses.kjv: But the path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.
verses.little_bible: The path of those who do right is like the sunrise — it starts with a little light and grows brighter and brighter all day long!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Doing right makes your path brighter and brighter.
verses.meaning: A life of righteousness grows in glory and light over time. Every wise choice adds more light until the full brightness of God's perfect day.
verses.memory_phrase: My path gets brighter every day.
verses.prayer: God, make my path shine brighter and brighter as I walk with You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you watched the sun rise? How does the light grow? How is that like the path of a good person?
verses.family_discussion: How has your family's faith grown brighter over time?
verses.do_it_today: Watch the sunrise or light a candle — think about how your life can grow brighter with each right choice.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking on a glowing golden path that gets brighter and wider as it stretches toward a beautiful sunrise

verses.kjv: The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.
verses.little_bible: But the path of wrong people is like walking in the dark — they keep tripping and cannot even see why.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The wrong path is dark. Those on it keep tripping and cannot see.
verses.meaning: Without wisdom and God's light, people stumble through life without understanding why things go wrong. Darkness has no clarity.
verses.memory_phrase: God's light shows me where to step.
verses.prayer: God, keep me in Your light so I always know where I am stepping.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you try to walk around in a dark room?
verses.family_discussion: How does God's Word act like a torch for our feet in the dark?
verses.do_it_today: Read Psalm 119:105 together: 'Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A comparison: one child walking in warm golden light, another stumbling on a dim tangled road

verses.kjv: My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.
verses.little_bible: My child, pay attention to my words. Lean your ear toward my teaching.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Pay close attention. Lean in and listen.
verses.meaning: Good teaching deserves our full, leaned-in attention. Wisdom is not for casual listening — it calls us to engage completely.
verses.memory_phrase: I lean in and listen carefully.
verses.prayer: God, help me lean in and really listen when You or a wise person is speaking.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between half-listening and really listening?
verses.family_discussion: How can our family become better listeners — to God and to each other?
verses.do_it_today: Put everything down during dinner and really listen to what each person says.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sitting up straight and leaning forward, listening with bright, focused eyes

verses.kjv: Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
verses.little_bible: Do not let these words disappear from your sight. Keep them deep in your heart.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep these words always in front of you and in your heart.
verses.meaning: Keeping wise words constantly in view and in the heart is how wisdom becomes part of who we are — not just something we know, but something we are.
verses.memory_phrase: I keep God's words in front of me.
verses.prayer: God, help me keep Your words always in front of my eyes and deep in my heart.
verses.discussion_question: How do you keep something important right in front of you so you don't forget it?
verses.family_discussion: How do you keep God's words in the centre of your home and family life?
verses.do_it_today: Put a Bible verse somewhere very visible in your home today — on the fridge or the front door.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a glowing verse card right in front of their eyes, and the same light glowing in their heart

verses.kjv: For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.
verses.little_bible: God's words are life to everyone who finds them, and they bring health to every part of you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's words give you life and make you healthy.
verses.meaning: Scripture is not just good advice — it is life-giving. God's words carry power that heals and sustains us from the inside out.
verses.memory_phrase: God's words give me life.
verses.prayer: God, let Your words be life to me — healing and health to my whole self.
verses.discussion_question: If the doctor gave you a medicine that made you totally healthy, would you take it? God's Word is like that!
verses.family_discussion: How has God's Word given life and health to your family in a real way?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse and say: 'This is life and health for me.' Believe it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Golden words flowing from a Bible like water, washing over a child who stands glowing with health and joy

verses.kjv: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
verses.little_bible: Guard your heart carefully — it is the most important thing to protect. Everything in your life flows out of it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Guard your heart. It is very important.
verses.meaning: What we fill our hearts with — what we love, watch, think, and feel — shapes everything we do and say. A clean heart makes a good life.
verses.memory_phrase: Guard your heart above all things.
verses.prayer: God, help me guard my heart and fill it with things that please You.
verses.discussion_question: What kinds of things can make your heart feel dirty or sad? What makes it feel clean and happy?
verses.family_discussion: What are we filling our hearts with as a family — through what we watch, listen to, and talk about?
verses.do_it_today: Before watching or listening to something, ask: 'Will this help my heart or hurt it?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing heart at the centre of a child's chest, with the child protectively holding their hands around it

verses.kjv: Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee.
verses.little_bible: Push away twisted, unkind words. Keep harmful and dishonest speech far from you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't say twisted or unkind things. Keep your words good.
verses.meaning: The words we speak reflect and shape the condition of our hearts. Guarding our heart means guarding what we say.
verses.memory_phrase: I speak kind and honest words.
verses.prayer: God, help me put away unkind and twisted words and speak only what is good.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of words that twist the truth or hurt people? How do those words make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: What kinds of words do we want our home to be full of? What kinds do we want to keep out?
verses.do_it_today: Before you speak today, ask: 'Are these words kind and true?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a gentle, warm smile choosing kind words that glow like little lights from their mouth

verses.kjv: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.
verses.little_bible: Keep your eyes looking straight ahead on the right path. Do not get distracted and look away.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep looking at the right path. Don't look away.
verses.meaning: Following God requires focus — we must fix our gaze on what is right and not let distractions or temptations pull us off course.
verses.memory_phrase: I keep my eyes on the right path.
verses.prayer: God, help me stay focused on what is right and not be distracted.
verses.discussion_question: What things try to distract you from doing the right thing?
verses.family_discussion: As a family, what distractions do we need to put down so we can focus on God?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one distraction to put away this evening and spend that time with God or your family instead.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking steadily on a bright path, eyes forward, with distractions to the side that they are not looking at

verses.kjv: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
verses.little_bible: Think carefully about the path your feet are on. Let all your ways be steady and sure.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Think about where you are walking. Make your ways steady.
verses.meaning: We are to be thoughtful, not impulsive — considering our direction and making sure our ways are established on solid ground.
verses.memory_phrase: I think about where my feet are going.
verses.prayer: God, help me think before I act and make all my ways steady and sure.
verses.discussion_question: Do you look where you are going before you step? How is that like thinking before you act?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to have ways that are 'established' — steady and solid?
verses.do_it_today: Before doing something today, pause for five seconds and think: 'Is this the right path?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child pausing thoughtfully at each step, looking carefully ahead at a glowing, well-defined path

verses.kjv: Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: remove thy foot from evil.
verses.little_bible: Do not turn to the right or the left — stay on God's path. Keep your foot away from evil.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Stay on God's path. Don't turn away from it.
verses.meaning: The right path requires staying on it — not drifting right or left, not being tempted to step off even a little. Remove every foot from evil.
verses.memory_phrase: I stay on God's path.
verses.prayer: God, help me stay exactly on Your path and not wander off to the side.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you start drifting off the right path just a little bit?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help each other stay on the right path as a family?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a straight path and put your name on it — commit to staying on God's path today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking a straight bright path, with encouraging arrows pointing forward, refusing to turn aside
